diagrams.description: Neuropsychology is the scientific study of brain‑behavior relationships with a focus on understanding cognitive, emotional, and behavioral consequences of brain dysfunction. This flowchart outlines its systematic approach: the ultimate goal is to diagnose and treat neuropsychological disorders by integrating knowledge of brain organization (localization, lateralization, networks) with clinical assessment. The process involves gathering data through neuropsychological tests, neuroimaging, and clinical interviews; interpreting findings within theoretical models (cognitive neuropsychology, functional systems); formulating diagnoses; and designing rehabilitation or intervention strategies. These steps are applied across clinical, forensic, and research settings, ultimately aiming to improve patient outcomes and advance neuroscience.
